folio.label: 64v
folio.l: ¢ How may this weyke womman , han this strengthe
Hire to defende , a geyn this renegat
O Golias , vn mesurable of lengthe
How mygút Dauyd , make the so mat
So ©onge , and of armure so desolat
How durst he loke , vp on thy dreedful face
Wel may men seen , it was but goddes grace
¢ Who ©af Iuditú , corage or hardynesse
To slen hi~ Olyfern~ , in his tent
And to delyueren out , of wrechednesse
The peeple of god , I sey for this entent
That rigút as god , spirit , of vigour sent
To hem , and saued he~ , out of meschaunce
So sent he mygút and vigour , to Custaunce
¢ Fortú gotú hire Ship~ , thurgú out the narugú moutú
Of Iubaltare and Septe , dryuyngö alway
Sumtyme west , & sumtyme Nortú & Soutú
And sumtyme Est , ful many a wery day
Til Cristes moder , blessed be she ay
Hatú shapen thurgú , hire endlees goodnesse
To make an ende , of al hire heuynesse
Now lat vs stynt , of Custaunce but a throwe
And speke we of the Romayn , Em¹our
That out of Surrye , hatú by &lbar;res knowe
The slaugútre of Cristene folk , and dishonour
Doon to his dougúter , by a fals traytour
I mene the cursed wykked , Soudanesse
That at the feest , let sleen bothe moore & lesse

note.notetxt: 861there is a sign in dry point next to the stanza.ODR

folio.l: ¢ For whicú this Em¹our , hatú sent a noon
His Senatour , witú roial ordinaunce
And othere lordes , god woot many oon
On Surryens , to taken heigú vengeaunce
They brennen , sleen , and brynge he~ to meschaunce
Ful many a day , but shortly this is the ende
Homward to Rome , they She~pen hem to wende
¢ This Senatour , repairetú witú victorie
To Romeward , seillynge ful roially
And mette the ship~ dryuyngö , as seitú the storie
In whicú Custaunce sittetú , ful pytously
No thyngö knew he , what she was , ne why
She was in swicú aray , ne she nyl sey
Of hire estate , thougú she shulde dey
¢ He bryngetú hire to Rome , and to his wyf´
He ©af hire , and hire ©ongö sone also
And witú the Senatour , she lad hire lif
